ddls:
CREATE TABLE "suspects" (
  "suspect_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suspect */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the suspect */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the suspect */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the suspect */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the suspect */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the suspect */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the suspect */,
  "known_aliases" TEXT /* Known aliases of the suspect */,
  "arrest_history" TEXT /* Brief history of the suspect's arrests */,
  "last_known_location" TEXT /* Last known location of the suspect */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the suspect (e.g., active, arrested, deceased) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suspect_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "letters" (
  "letter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each letter */,
  "suspect_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the suspect who sent the letter */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date when the letter was sent */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the letter */,
  "is_analyzed" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the letter has been analyzed */,
  "sender_name" TEXT /* Name of the sender if different from the suspect */,
  "recipient_name" TEXT /* Name of the recipient */,
  "recipient_email" TEXT /* Email address of the recipient */,
  "subject" TEXT /* Subject of the letter */,
  "attachment_count" INTEGER /* Number of attachments in the letter */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the letter (e.g., email, mail, social media) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("letter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_letters_suspect_id FOREIGN KEY ("suspect_id") REFERENCES suspects ("suspect_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "letter_frequency" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frequency entry */,
  "letter" TEXT /* The letter being analyzed */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency percentage of the letter in the suspect's letters */,
  "suspect_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the suspect associated with this frequency */,
  "letter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific letter being analyzed */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the frequency analysis was performed */,
  "total_letters" INTEGER /* Total number of letters analyzed for the suspect */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_letter_frequency_letter_id FOREIGN KEY ("letter_id") REFERENCES letters ("letter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_letter_frequency_suspect_id FOREIGN KEY ("suspect_id") REFERENCES suspects ("suspect_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investigators" (
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investigator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the investigator */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the investigator (e.g., detective, analyst) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the investigator */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the investigator belongs to */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank or seniority of the investigator */,
  "date_hired" TEXT /* Date the investigator was hired */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the investigator (e.g., basic, advanced, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("investigator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the investigator accessing the data */,
  "letter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the letter being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_investigator_id FOREIGN KEY ("investigator_id") REFERENCES investigators ("investigator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_letter_id FOREIGN KEY ("letter_id") REFERENCES letters ("letter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* The keyword of interest */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the keyword's relevance */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the keyword (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the keyword (e.g., violent, financial, narcotics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)
